task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Follow-up Visit' event definition has been removed.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the OpenClinica Event Definitions or Build Study page, 'Follow-up Visit' should not be listed among the active event definitions.

task_completion.description: Verify that the 'Baseline Assessment' event definition is still present.
task_completion.visual_evidence: On the OpenClinica Event Definitions page, 'Baseline Assessment' should be clearly visible and active in the list of events.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Week 4 Safety Assessment' event definition.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The event 'Week 4 Safety Assessment' is listed in the Event Definitions table or shown in a creation form. It should be configured as 'Scheduled' and non-repeating.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Week 12 Efficacy Evaluation' event definition.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The event 'Week 12 Efficacy Evaluation' is listed in the Event Definitions table or shown in a creation form. It should be configured as 'Scheduled' and non-repeating.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'Unscheduled Safety Visit' event definition.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The event 'Unscheduled Safety Visit' is listed in the Event Definitions table or shown in a creation form. It should be configured as 'Unscheduled' and repeating.

task_completion.description: Verify the creation of the 'End of Treatment Visit' event definition.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The event 'End of Treatment Visit' is listed in the Event Definitions table or shown in a creation form. It should be configured as 'Scheduled' and non-repeating.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent interacted with the OpenClinica web interface.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the OpenClinica web application in a browser, with genuine UI elements like navigation bars, study selection dropdowns, and tables.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not use terminal commands or scripts to bypass the UI.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing database SQL injections, scripts, or curl commands to the OpenClinica backend.

integrity.description: Verify that the event descriptions and details were entered into the OpenClinica forms.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the agent filling out the 'Create Event Definition' forms or the final table reflecting the newly added events natively in the OpenClinica UI.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target study: 'Phase II Diabetes Trial' (DM-TRIAL-2024). Event to remove: 'Follow-up Visit'. Event to keep: 'Baseline Assessment'. Exactly four new events must be created: 1. 'Week 4 Safety Assessment' (Scheduled, non-repeating), 2. 'Week 12 Efficacy Evaluation' (Scheduled, non-repeating), 3. 'Unscheduled Safety Visit' (Unscheduled, repeating), 4. 'End of Treatment Visit' (Scheduled, non-repeating).
